examples.text: وَيُسْقَوْنَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا زَنْجَبِيلًا عَيْنًا فِيهَا تُسَمَّى سَلْسَبِيلًا
examples.english: There are they watered with a cup whereof the mixture is of ginger and the spring bearing the name of Salsabīl

senses.gloss: a copious source of nourishment, used to aggrandize the contents of a book or a restaurant for marketing purposes, in reference to the Qurʔānic description of Paradise

etymology.text: Probably an elistic name of Aramaic and ultimately Akkadian origin. For metrical considerations it could still have tanwīn in spite of being, as a proper noun, diptote.
